notes: Default gravity deck for Dante's office. Pressure beats (the bell rings, a folder is misplaced) plus warm beats (the clerk has it, the precedent lands). The brass clock ticks under every card.

deck.title: the bell rings
deck.flavor: the bell above the front door rings. somebody you weren't expecting. the bell is older than the office; dante's father bought it used.

deck.title: the clerk has it
deck.flavor: before you ask, the clerk hands you the folder. she has been doing this since before you knew what to ask for.

deck.title: the radiator clicks
deck.flavor: the radiator does its small percussive thing. the room steadies. dante's shoulders drop a quarter-inch.

deck.title: a petitioner changes their mind
deck.flavor: somebody in the anteroom puts on their coat and leaves. they will be back next week or they will not be back. either of those things is fine.

deck.title: the stamp wears thin
deck.flavor: the ink pad needs reinking. you do it at the desk with the small bottle from the drawer. the bottle has been the same bottle for two years.

deck.title: the clock corrects itself
deck.flavor: the brass desk clock chimes a half-tone off. it has not done that in years. dante looks at it. he does not adjust it. the clock corrects on its own within the minute.

deck.title: a misfiled folder appears
deck.flavor: in the precedent shelf, a folder that belongs in estate. dante's father filed it wrong in 1971. the case is the case the appellant came in for.

deck.title: outside, a small protest
deck.flavor: two voices on the bank, raised. their case is not in this office; it is in the office two doors up the street. they will be heard there. they will not be heard here. dante notes it anyway.

deck.title: a signature doesn't match
deck.flavor: the page in front of you has the petitioner's name in two different hands. one of them is the petitioner's. the other one is not. dante sets the page aside.

deck.title: the day ticks forward
deck.flavor: you stamp something. the ledger ticks. it's the kind of small ruling that always lands — a name change, a confirmation, a piece of paperwork that just needed somebody to say yes.

deck.title: the clerk corrects you
deck.flavor: she clears her throat. you are at the wrong volume on the shelf. she has been clearing her throat at you for nineteen years and you have not yet failed to look up.

deck.title: the office warms through
deck.flavor: the radiator finally gets the corner where the cabinet meets the wall. the bronze of the heavy stamps has been cold all morning. it isn't anymore.

deck.title: the appeals case arrives
deck.flavor: the appellate brief is on the desk. you did not put it there. the clerk did not put it there. you can see in the clerk's posture that she did not put it there.

deck.title: the clock runs long
deck.flavor: 5:14 PM. the bell rang at five. the bell has not rung again. the anteroom is empty. the brass clock keeps ticking. it has been quarter past five for what feels like a longer minute than the office should have.

deck.title: the office settles
deck.flavor: everything that needed to be filed is filed. the cabinet is in order. the clerk has her coat on the hook ready. the room agrees that today was a good day.
